task.title: Implement Git-backed archival GC and restore contract
task.summary: Use implementation builds as publication payloads, Git refs/trailers as cold ledger pointers, and compact CodeWiki hot state for closed task/sprint recovery without graph bloat.

goal.outcome: Closed tasks and sprints can be safely moved out of hot CodeWiki state after validated implementation-build publication metadata, while Git refs/trailers/digests allow explicit restore or refinement later.
acceptance: Implementation builds expose publication metadata for archive refs, commit trailers, artifact digests, restore commands, and push safety status without introducing a new capsule artifact.
GC gates only mark builds/tasks purgeable after validation, digest capture, archive ledger entry, and publication safety checks pass.
Graph/status default views represent cold artifacts with compact ledger refs instead of expanded historical node/edge sets, while hot active work and blockers remain fully routable.

Restore flow can locate an archived task/sprint from compact ledger data and hydrate a bounded context from Git using refs/worktrees/sparse paths without making restored history current truth automatically.
Security policy blocks remote push of CodeWiki artifacts until secret scanning, remote visibility, and fail/block/private evidence handling pass.
Tests cover implementation-build publication payload fields, GC gating, compact graph cold refs, and restore lookup behavior.
non_goals: No new archive capsule artifact or lexicon.
No treating Git namespaces as an access-control boundary.
No automatic remote push without explicit approval/policy pass.
No broad rewrite of roadmap/task identity or compiler loops.
No storing large raw evidence blobs in default graph/status output.
verification: codewiki_state refresh=true shows compact hot/cold GC view and no unresolved drift

summary: Implemented Git-backed archival GC and restore metadata using implementation builds as publication payloads.

evidence: Extended existing build/validation feature test instead of adding broad fixtures.
Asserted restore and GC metadata through generated graph only; no large payload expansion required.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Implementation build writer computes bounded SHA-256 artifact digests and publication restore metadata.
Graph builder compacts cold validated implementation builds behind archive refs and exposes restore index/gates.
notes: No new capsule artifact or lexicon added; implementation_build remains the publication payload.
Remote push remains blocked by default until explicit safety fields pass.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Extended existing build/validation feature test instead of adding broad fixtures.
Asserted restore and GC metadata through generated graph only; no large payload expansion required.
code_change_evidence: Implementation build writer computes bounded SHA-256 artifact digests and publication restore metadata.
Graph builder compacts cold validated implementation builds behind archive refs and exposes restore index/gates.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Implementation builds expose publication metadata for archive refs, commit trailers, artifact digests, restore commands, and push safety status without introducing a new capsule artifact.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: writeImplementationBuild now emits publication.git, publication.archive_ledger, publication.artifact_digests, commit trailers, restore metadata, and push_readiness.security using the implementation_build artifact.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: GC gates only mark builds/tasks purgeable after validation, digest capture, archive ledger entry, and publication safety checks pass.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: graph.ts computes purgeable builds/tasks through isPurgeableByGitArchive(validated + archive ledger + digest capture + safe_to_push).

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Graph/status default views represent cold artifacts with compact ledger refs instead of expanded historical node/edge sets, while hot active work and blockers remain fully routable.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: graph.ts compacts cold validated implementation builds with archive refs, adds git_archive_ref nodes and gc.restore_index, and skips expanded code/test/validation/closure edges for compact cold builds.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Restore flow can locate an archived task/sprint from compact ledger data and hydrate a bounded context from Git using refs/worktrees/sparse paths without making restored history current truth automatically.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: publication.git.restore and graph.views.gc.restore_index expose /wiki-restore command, git worktree command, git show build command, and sparse_paths with note that restored history is reference material.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Security policy blocks remote push of CodeWiki artifacts until secret scanning, remote visibility, and fail/block/private evidence handling pass.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: publication.push_readiness defaults safe_to_push=false and records required secret_scan, remote_visibility, private_evidence checks plus git_namespaces not_access_control.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tests cover implementation-build publication payload fields, GC gating, compact graph cold refs, and restore lookup behavior.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: tests/smoke/builds.test.mjs asserts publication git fields, trailers, digest, blocked push readiness, gc.restore_index, cold archive refs, and blocked purge gate.

closure_brief.user_intent: Use Git more efficiently as CodeWiki cold ledger while keeping CodeWiki hot state small, with implementation builds serving as the disciplined push package and security gates for Git/GitHub safety.
implemented_changes: Added implementation-build publication metadata for Git archive refs, commit trailers, digests, restore commands, and push safety.
Added graph GC restore index and Git archive gates for compact cold artifact representation.
Added tests covering publication payload, restore lookup, cold archive refs, and blocked purge gating.
Kept remote push recommendation-only and blocked by default.
acceptance_evidence: All TASK-068 acceptance criteria mapped in implementation build acceptance_mapping.
remaining_risks: Executable restore/push commands remain follow-up tooling; current slice defines and tests metadata/gates.

risks: Actual /wiki-restore and remote push commands are metadata contracts in this slice; executable restore/push tools can be added as follow-up if needed.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

commit.body:
Implemented Git-backed archival GC and restore metadata using implementation builds as publication payloads.

Checks: npm run typecheck, npm run test:features, npm run test:smoke, npm run check:architecture, npm run test:pack, npm test

Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
